evals.name: initial-setup
evals.prompt: I just provisioned a fresh Ubuntu 24.04 VPS on Hetzner (public IP: 203.0.113.50). I want to install k3s and be able to manage it from my MacBook. Walk me through the setup.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step k3s installation with remote access configuration

assertions.text: Uses curl install script with specific version pin (INSTALL_K3S_VERSION)
Copies kubeconfig to local machine and updates server address from 127.0.0.1 to public IP

evals.name: add-nodes
evals.prompt: I have a single-node k3s cluster on node0 (Ubuntu 24.04, x86-64). I want to add node1 as a second control plane node and node2 as a worker-only node. node2 is ARM64. How do I do this?
evals.expected_output: Procedures for adding both server and agent nodes to existing k3s cluster

assertions.text: Retrieves join token from the correct location
Joins server node with server subcommand and --server flag pointing to node0
Matches original install flags (--disable=traefik, --cluster-domain if set)
Joins agent node with agent subcommand (distinct from server join)
Pins INSTALL_K3S_VERSION to match existing cluster
Confirms mixed architecture (ARM64 + x86-64) works without special config

evals.name: kubectl-essentials
evals.prompt: I'm new to kubectl. I have k3s running and my app is deployed. Show me the essential commands I need for daily management -- checking status, viewing logs, debugging pods, and basic operations.
evals.expected_output: kubectl command reference covering daily operations

assertions.text: Shows kubectl get for pods, deployments, services
Shows kubectl exec for interactive shell access
